name: Text Classification Pipeline
overview: End-to-end pipeline from raw text to a deployed classifier. Start with a TF-IDF + LogisticRegression baseline, then escalate to BERT fine-tuning only if the baseline F1 is below 0.85.

steps.name: Data Loading and Class Audit
steps.what: Load raw text data and inspect class distribution, text lengths, and null rates before any preprocessing.
steps.decision: If the largest class exceeds 60% of the data, apply class_weight='balanced' in the classifier or use stratified oversampling before training. Never skip this step — imbalance masks real model failures behind high accuracy.
failure_points: Majority-class collapse: accuracy looks fine but macro-F1 is near zero because the model predicts only the dominant class.
Null text rows silently dropped without logging — downstream column counts no longer match the original dataset.

steps.name: Preprocessing
steps.what: Normalize and clean text. Apply only what the downstream model needs — different models have different preprocessing requirements.
steps.decision: Lowercase + strip punctuation for TF-IDF baselines. Preserve original casing for BERT fine-tuning — the cased tokenizer encodes 'US' and 'us' differently. Never apply both in the same pipeline.
failure_points: Applying TF-IDF-style lowercasing to BERT input — the cased tokenizer treats 'Apple' and 'apple' as different tokens; lowercasing silently discards this signal.
Preprocessing the full dataset before train/test split — stopword removal or normalization based on dataset statistics leaks into the test set.

steps.name: Feature Extraction
steps.what: Convert text to numerical features suitable for the classifier.
steps.decision: TF-IDF for datasets under 50K samples with clear keyword signals. Dense embeddings (sentence-transformers) for datasets above 50K or where paraphrase equivalence matters more than keyword overlap.
failure_points: Fitting TfidfVectorizer on the full dataset before splitting — test set vocabulary leaks into the vectorizer's idf weights.
Embedding model max sequence length (typically 512 tokens) silently truncates long documents — verify with tokenizer(text, truncation=False) on your longest examples.

steps.name: Baseline Model: TF-IDF + LogisticRegression
steps.what: Fit a fast, interpretable baseline and record its macro-F1 score as the threshold for model escalation.
steps.decision: If macro-F1 >= 0.85 on the validation set, ship the baseline — it is production-ready and much cheaper to serve than BERT. Escalate to fine-tuning only if below 0.85 and you have at least 1000 labeled examples per class.
failure_points: Using accuracy as the evaluation metric on imbalanced data — always report macro-F1 alongside accuracy.
Omitting stratify=y in train_test_split — small minority classes can end up entirely in one split.

steps.name: BERT Fine-Tuning (Escalation Path)
steps.what: Fine-tune a pretrained transformer on the classification task when the TF-IDF baseline is insufficient.
steps.decision: Use distilbert-base-uncased for latency-constrained deployments. Use bert-base-uncased or roberta-base for higher accuracy with less constraint. Freeze the first 6 encoder layers if dataset is under 2000 examples to prevent catastrophic forgetting.
failure_points: Overfitting on small datasets without early stopping — BERT can reach near-zero training loss in 2 epochs on 500 examples while validation F1 collapses.
Label IDs not matching num_labels in AutoModelForSequenceClassification — causes silent shape mismatch in the classification head output.

steps.name: Evaluation and Export
steps.what: Evaluate on the held-out test set, inspect confusion matrix, and serialize the final pipeline.
steps.decision: Use joblib for sklearn Pipelines — it preserves vectorizer + classifier as a single artifact. Use save_pretrained for HuggingFace models — it exports both tokenizer and weights together. Never export them separately.
failure_points: Exporting model weights without the tokenizer — the two are inseparable at inference time; save both to the same directory.
Testing on validation set instead of a held-out test set — if you used validation F1 for model selection, it is no longer an unbiased estimate.

evaluation_checks: Macro-F1 on held-out test set, not validation set used for model selection
Per-class F1 reported — not just weighted or accuracy
Confusion matrix inspected for systematic misclassification between specific class pairs
common_failure_points: Class imbalance where the majority class dominates accuracy but macro-F1 collapses, giving a false impression of model quality.
Label leakage from including metadata in the text that correlates with the target but will not be available at inference time.
Tokenization mismatch between TF-IDF vectorizer and BERT tokenizer, causing the same word to be split differently and producing incompatible feature spaces.
Overfitting on small datasets when using BERT without early stopping or data augmentation, resulting in high training accuracy but poor generalization.
Evaluation data leakage through preprocessing steps like stopword removal or TF-IDF fitting on the entire dataset before splitting, which silently biases the test set.
